message:
fix: remove destructive eager delete_all() in --full rebuild path

The --full rebuild path called t_index.delete_all() + commit() before
the scan/rebuild operations. If anything failed afterward (OOM on large
databases, UNIQUE constraint errors during historical salvage, etc.),
the Tantivy index was left completely empty with 0 segments and no
automatic recovery.

This deletion was redundant: rebuild_tantivy_from_db() always runs at
the end of a successful --full rebuild and atomically removes + recreates
the index directory from the authoritative SQLite database. Removing the
eager delete_all() means that on failure, the prior (stale but non-empty)
index remains intact, giving users a functional fallback.

Fixes #164

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
